texto: Actively, we are motivated by the never-ending desires of the self. We are compelled to pursue whatever the self imagines will satisfy its desires. We are convinced that satisfaction of desire is the source of true happiness. Yet the very nature of desire does not permit happiness. Like trying to quench one's thirst by drinking salt water, satisfying desire only stimulates the flow of desire. In the wake of fulfillment, desire once more stirs and reaches out. There is never lasting satisfaction, not even completion.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dharma Publishing: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Actively, we are motivated by the never-ending desires of the self. We are compelled to pursue wh…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, buddhism, desire pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e desire.

frase_original: Actively, we are motivated by the never-ending desires of the self. We are compelled to pursue whatever the self imagines will satisfy its desires. We are convinced that satisfaction of desire is the source of true happiness. Yet the very nature of desire does not permit happiness. Like trying to quench one's thirst by drinking salt water, satisfying desire only stimulates the flow of desire. In the wake of fulfillment, desire once more stirs and reaches out. There is never lasting satisfaction, not even completion.

texto: Both outer and inner phenomena arise as a result of causes and conditions. Outer phenomena, the things of the physical world, arise in a series of seven steps. The texts use the example of a seed giving rise to a plant that gives rise to a fruit. The seven steps are: seed, sprout, leaflets, stemmed plant, bud, flower, fruit. Each stage succeeds the previous one in time and in order, each giving rise to the next.
explicacao: Citação de Dharma Publishing sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Both outer and inner phenomena arise as a result of causes and conditions. Outer phenomena, the t…” articula tempo, buddhism, interdependent em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como termino e estudos.
